pullRequest.title: Add submitter to roles being emailed on ticket update
pullRequest.body:
Rationalize the email notifications handling into a single method call.
Add ability to enable sending email notifications to submitter via setting for all changes to ticket.

pullRequest.title: Make the "Copies To" field in the ticket edit screen readonly when email notifications are not enabled in Queue
pullRequest.body: Checks the Queue.enable_notifications_on_email_events field setting and shows the "Copies To" field as disabled if the queue notifications field is not checked Fixes #914 This is how it looks when CC's are disabled and includes the hover text when the mouse hovers on the disabled button:

pullRequest.title: Fix cc crash when CC email address is assocaited with multiple active User records
Fix markdown javascript injection prevention
pullRequest.body:
Fixes #1102 

There ended up being some fairly significant shrotcomings in the orioginal algorithm and have now added more detailed tests to reflect the capability.

pullRequest.title: BUG: Limit title length in edit ticket form
Fix due_date being overwritten by ticket update shortcuts
pullRequest.body:
resolves #1281

This implementation is safe because [updating the ticket from the form](https://github.com/django-helpdesk/django-helpdesk/blob/main/helpdesk/views/staff.py#L671) already cleans the due_date from the form. When due_date is None, it will be set to the existing ticket.due_date anyways.

pullRequest.title: Migrations made reversible
pullRequest.body:
This resolves #1291 

Some migrations are not reversible, which makes it impossible to uninstall the application in a "django way". I have added a "no_op" function for migration 19 to make it reversible. As a matter of fact, this migration calls a function to clean up the ticket's secret key. But reversing it will remove the column, so there is no need to add information to the underlying column because of it.

Migration 20 depickles an old column that had settings pickled. So, the reverse method is to pickle that back again, although I doubt that someone would want to undo this particular migration. But, for the sake of consistency, it is important to have it undoing whatever is the operation that was carried out.

I have tested it by migrating and "unmigrating", one migration at a time and the whole thing at once, to be sure both migrations are working as expected.

pullRequest.title: Fix crash in UpdateTicketView when unauthenticated user logs in after redirect
pullRequest.body:
### Problem
When an unauthenticated user tries to add a comment/resolution, they are redirected to login. After login, the redirect caused a 500 error because UpdateTicketView accessed form.data on a GET request.

### Solution
Added a safe check in get_context_data to only access form.data if it exists. Otherwise, provide an empty dict.

### Tests
Added regression test to confirm:
- Unauthenticated users are redirected to login.
- After login, the update page loads successfully (status 200).

### Related Issue
Closes #1315

### Future Work
It might be possible to preserve the entered comment/attachments across login using session storage or temporary persistence. This PR does not address that, but I’d be happy to explore it in a follow-up if maintainers are interested.

pullRequest.title: Fix missing custom fields in UpdateTicketView after validation errors
pullRequest.body:
### Problem
When a ticket has custom fields, they display correctly on the first load (`/tickets/<id>/` via `view_ticket`).  
However, if invalid data is submitted on the update form, the page reloads with errors but the custom fields disappear.  
This happens because `UpdateTicketView` did not provide `customfields_form` to the template, unlike `view_ticket`.

### Solution
- Updated `UpdateTicketView.get_context_data` to always pass `customfields_form` (using `EditTicketCustomFieldForm`).  
- This ensures that custom fields remain visible both on initial GET and after POST with validation errors.

### Tests
Added a regression test:
  - Creates a custom field.
  - Confirms it is visible on the initial view (`helpdesk:view`).
  - Submits invalid data to `helpdesk:update`.
  - Confirms the page shows validation errors *and* the custom field is still visible.

### Related Issue
Closes #1318
